title: Using projects in ChatGPT
optimized_headline: Unlocking Project Management with ChatGPT: What You Need to Know
raw_excerpt: Learn how to use orojects in ChatGPT to organize chats, files, and instructions, manage ongoing work, and collaborate more effectively.
raw_body: Learn how to use orojects in ChatGPT to organize chats, files, and instructions, manage ongoing work, and collaborate more effectively.
speedrun: ChatGPT has introduced a feature called projects, designed to help users organize their chats, files, and instructions. This new functionality allows for better management of ongoing work and enhances collaboration among users. By structuring conversations and resources, teams can streamline their workflow. This matters now as remote collaboration becomes increasingly important in various sectors.
why_it_matters: Teams can benefit from improved organization, making it easier to track progress and share information effectively.
This feature reflects a broader trend towards integrated tools that enhance collaboration in remote work environments.

lenses.eli12: Projects in ChatGPT help you keep your conversations and files neat and tidy. Think of it like organizing a toolbox, where each tool has its place. This organization makes it easier for everyone to find what they need, which is especially helpful when working from home or in teams.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, the introduction of projects in ChatGPT addresses a common user need for better organization in collaborative settings. By improving efficiency in managing tasks and resources, teams can save time and reduce miscommunication. This could lead to faster project completion and enhanced productivity.
lenses.engineer: The projects feature in ChatGPT likely involves a structured data model that categorizes chats and files, enabling efficient retrieval and organization. This could be built on existing conversational AI frameworks, enhancing user experience through improved context management. However, specific technical details on the underlying architecture weren't provided.
